Wtfiflm-mfiWW2007—08QFWufififimlfigfififiWaTfi? 3:11me mmemmmgwmaaammmm frméflrqftanfivmflsmw, Wmamimme -mmamameWm$m-a-mwmmfimmmm fiq’mmmemsmmmemammmmw newmmmmmmemwteqwan-mfimmmmmemaemememmmmafimmi ' [1171. 11' m/13013/707/niafi/10 unfini— 21211311 (1120— 265.0)313113] , We emf again nfaa MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS NOTIFICATION New Delhi the 3lstJuly, 2013 5.0 B_2_6(E).-—Whereas by notification of the Government of India inthe Ministry of Road Transport and I Highways number 5.0 1667 (E), dated the 24th July, 2012, issued under section 11 of the National Highways 1 . Authority of India Act, 1988 ( 68 of 1988), the Central Government has entrusted the stretch from kilometer 0.000 . to kilometer 265 000 (Cuttack- Sambalpur section) of National Highway No'. 42 in the State of Odisha to the National Highways Authority of India (hereinafter referred to as the ”Authority”); - And whereas, pursuant to the provision of section 14 of the National Highways Authority of India Act, 1988 (68 , of 1988), the Authority has entered into an Agreement on 13. 03. 2012 (hereinafter referred to as said agreement) . with M/s Abhijeet Angul Sambalpur Toll Road Limited, having its registered office at EN 1, Insignia Tower 3rd Floor, Sector V, Salt Lake, Kolkata — 700091, (hereinafter referred to as the ”Concessionaire”), for bid submitted on 30.08.2011 for the development of Angul — Sambalpurflsection from Km 112.000 to Km 265.000 on National Highway No. 42 (hereinafter referred to as the ”said section”) to four lane and operation and maintenance of the said section. -- 4 of 6 -- [9m [Flame 300] ‘ ' we EH! 113m : 31mm 5 Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 8A of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956), read with rule 3 of the National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules, 2008 published vide G.S.R 838 (E) dated 5th December, 2008 and amended vide G.S.R 950 (E) dated 3rd December, 2010 and G.S.R 15 )E) dated 12th January, 2011 (hereinafter referred to as the ”Rules”), the Central Government, having regard .to the expenditure involved in building, maintenance, management and operation of the said section, interest on the capital invested, reasonable return, the volume of traffic and the period of the Agreement entered into between the Authority and the Concessionaire‘, hereby notifies that there shall be levied and collected fees on mechanical vehicles for the use of the said section at the rate specified in columns (2) of the Table 1 for net road section length of 146.958 Km for use of the four lane sections ((hereinafter referred to as ”base rate") and at one and half times the base rate for bypass having length 3.900 Km on the ‘Type of Vehicles specified in Table 1 below, and authorizes the said Concessionaire to Collect and retain the fee on and from the date of Commercial Operation (COD) declared by the Authority and on getting the Completion Certificate or the provisional Completion Certificate, as the case may be, from the authority or publication of this notification - in the Official Gazette, whichever is later, till the termination date as specified in the agreement; I TABLE 1 V , . . Type of vehicle ' Base rate of fee per km for the base year 2007438 (in Rupees) (1) (2) Car, Jeep, Van or Light Motor Vehicle . 0.65 Light Commercial Vehicle, Light Goods Vehicle or Mini Bus 1.05 Bus or Truck (Two Axles) 2.20 Three — axle commercial vehicles ' 2.40 Heavy Construction Machinery (HCM) or Earth Moving Equipment 3.45 (EME) or Multi Axle Vehicle (MAV) ( four to six axles) Oversized Vehicles (seven 'or more axles) 1 4.20 Note: 1. The fee levied and collected hereunder shall be due and payable at the following Toll Plaza for the distance specified for such Toll Plaza(s): " A Location of Toll Plaza(s) (chainage) Lenfli (in km) for which Fee is payable Km /Ch. 182.000 of NH 42, 80000 Near Harirajpur Village in Aungul district, - Km/ Ch. 244.500 of NH 42, 66.958 Near Hatibari Village in Sambalpur district. 2. In additional to above, the following fee shall also be due and payable for the use of the following bypass(es) at the following toll plaza at one and half times of the base rate for the length of bypass specified for such toll plaza: Location of Toll Plaza(s) (chainage) Length of bypass (in km) for which additional fee is payable Km/ Ch. 244.500 of NH 42, Sambalpur Bypass of 3.900 Km length and costing Rs.22.65 crore (taking off at Km 2581958 and joining at Near Hatibari Village in Sambalpur district. Km 262.858 of NH—42) 3. The rate of monthly pass for a person who owns a mechanical vehicle registered for non— commercial purposes and resides within a distance of twenty kilometers from the toll plaza is_Rs. 215.00 (Rupees Two Hundred Fifteen only) for the year 2013-14 which is subject to revision every year as per the provrsrons of the National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules, 2008. 333:: 62/5? -- 5 of 6 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART ll- Sizc. 3(ii)] 6. Rule, shall be liable to pay fee at such rates which is applicable for the next higher category of the vehicles and shall not be entitled to make use of the highway section unless the excess load has been removed from such mechanical vehicle. 7. serviceroad, alternative road etc. shall be as per definitions specified in the Rules. 3. 9. of fee to be charged from the mechanical vehicles and the discounts will be calculated by the authority on the sasis of the completed portion of the length of the Project Highway The same shall be got published by the Authority through the Concessionaire in at least one newspaper, each in English and vernacular language and therebfter revised annually and published in accordance with the Rule 5 of the said rules and provision of the Concbssion Agreement provided that no revision shall be effected within a period of Six months froflthe date of .he preceding revision of fee. .
